title: Flagship LLMs Under $20: Compare Prices Grid
description: Three premium flagships at less than $20 per 1M input tokens — Opus 4, GPT-5, and Grok 4 — gridded in TokenRate's Compare Prices.

sections.heading: What "Flagship Under $20" Means in 2026
sections.body: The [Compare Prices tool](/tools/compare-prices) is the fastest way to put a shortlist of LLMs in a single grid — input cost, output cost, context window, and quality score in stacked columns you can scan vertically. Provider dropdowns let you mix models across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Meta, DeepSeek, Mistral, and xAI without leaving the page. Even within the "Over $10" premium bracket, the spread between Claude Opus 4 at $15 and OpenAI o3 at $10 is significant — the Compare Prices grid surfaces it cleanly. The [Compare Prices view](/tools/compare-prices) makes the "Flagship Under $20" picks visible at a glance — once you've ticked the candidates, the input-cost column does the budget filtering visually. Pair this with [flagship/balanced/fast/reasoning LLM tiers](/blog/flagship-balanced-fast-reasoning-llm-tiers), [Arena AI leaderboard Elo scores explained](/blog/arena-ai-leaderboard-elo-scores-explained), and [how LLM quality scores are calculated](/blog/how-llm-quality-scores-are-calculated).

sections.heading: Who Fits the "Flagship Under $20" Bucket
sections.body: **Claude Opus 4** (Anthropic): $15.00 / $75.00, Q85, 200K ctx. **GPT-5** (OpenAI): $1.25 / $10.00, Q82, 200K ctx. **Grok 4** (xAI): $3.00 / $15.00, Q79, 256K ctx. **OpenAI o3** (OpenAI): $10.00 / $40.00, Q86, 200K ctx. All ticked together in [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices), the grid lays out the tradeoffs: quality varies from 79 to 86 within the budget, and output costs span $10.00 to $75.00/1M. Pick the highest-Value (quality ÷ input cost) entry that meets your quality floor.

sections.heading: Why Cheapest ≠ Best
sections.body: Within the "Flagship Under $20" bucket, the cheapest model is rarely the best Value pick. A model at $0.20 input / quality 60 has Value = 300; a model at $0.50 input / quality 70 has Value = 140 — the cheaper model wins by 2.1×. But if your workload's quality floor is 65, the cheaper model is disqualified even before Value enters the discussion. Always set the quality floor first, then optimize Value within it. For the framework, see [why the cheapest LLM isn't always the best value](/blog/why-cheapest-llm-isnt-always-best-value).

sections.heading: The Flagship Under $20 Picks, Side-by-Side
sections.body: In [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices), tick all candidates across their provider dropdowns. The grid renders input, output, context, and quality in stacked columns. For most Flagship Under $20 workloads, the order of operations is: quality floor first, then Value, then output cost. The grid makes all three visible in one scan. If your monthly volume is small (< 10M tokens), the model differences won't make material bill impact — pick on quality. If volume is high (> 1B tokens/month), even small input-cost deltas compound.

sections.heading: From Budget Bucket to Production
sections.body: Once you've shortlisted within the Flagship Under $20 bucket, run the candidates through [/tools/api-cost-estimator](/tools/api-cost-estimator) with your real workload volume to project monthly cost. For ongoing budget control, instrument token usage in production so you can catch cost regressions early — see [token usage auditing](/blog/token-usage-auditing-find-hidden-costs). Both the price denominator (OpenRouter) and the quality numerator (Arena AI + Artificial Analysis) refresh hourly. So the comparison you screenshot Monday morning is still trustworthy at standup Tuesday morning — but you should re-run it before a quarterly model-routing review. Run the comparison live at [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices), then bookmark the URL for next month's price audit.

faq.question: How do I open the Compare Prices grid?
faq.answer: Two ways: click the 'Compare Prices' tab at the top of the calculator card on the home page, or navigate directly to /tools/compare-prices. The standalone page is also linked from the main navigation under 'Tools'.

faq.question: Can I share my comparison with teammates?
faq.answer: Yes — the page URL captures the current state. Send the link in Slack and your teammate sees the same grid. Useful for procurement and architecture-review meetings.

faq.question: Is the data live or cached?
faq.answer: Live from OpenRouter (prices) and a blended Arena AI + Artificial Analysis pipeline (quality), refreshed on a 60-minute incremental cache. So the grid is at most an hour stale.

faq.question: Where do I go after the grid to project monthly cost?
faq.answer: Once you've picked a winner, go to /tools/api-cost-estimator and plug in the model + your expected monthly token volume. The estimator does the per-1M math against your real workload mix.

ctaText: Run the comparison live at [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices), then bookmark the URL for next month's price audit.
